categories.label: Mechanical hazards
categories.description: Mechanical hazards from moving parts, hazardous surfaces, transport equipment, pressurised media, or falling objects.
examples: parts with hazardous surfaces (edges, corners, points, blades, roughness)
moving vehicles/transport gear (impact points, tipping, run-over, falls from ramps/slopes)
pinch, shear, impact, cutting, piercing, in-running nip, entanglement points
falling objects (workpieces, tools dropping from platforms/scaffolds)

categories.label: Fall hazards
categories.description: Fall hazards from working at height, floor openings, steps, slippery surfaces, clutter, visibility, ladders, ramps, or lift platforms.

categories.label: Electrical hazards
categories.description: Electrical hazards from live parts, electrostatic discharge, short circuits, overloads, arc flashes, or work on live systems.
examples: short circuits, overloads, arc flashes, etc.

categories.label: Harmful substances (chemical / biological)
categories.description: Harmful substances include chemical and biological gases, vapours, liquids, aerosols, solids, dusts, and microorganisms.
examples: toxic, carcinogenic, mutagenic, reprotoxic, irritant, corrosive agents; microorganisms (viruses, bacteria, parasites, fungi, cell cultures, GMOs)

categories.label: Fire and explosion hazards
categories.description: Fire and explosion hazards from flammable liquids, dusts, gases, solids, explosive atmospheres, explosive substances, or ignition sources.

categories.label: Thermal hazards
categories.description: Thermal hazards from hot or cold media, surfaces, liquids, steam, refrigerants, or material splashes.
examples: hot/cold media (open flames, hot/cold surfaces or fluids, steam, refrigerants, splashes)

categories.label: Specific physical agents
categories.description: Specific physical agents include noise, ultrasound, infrasound, radiation, electromagnetic fields, underpressure, or overpressure.
examples: non-ionising radiation (UV curing, arc welding, sun exposure, lasers, electromagnetic fields)

categories.label: Environmental conditions
categories.description: Environmental-condition hazards include climate, weather, humidity, heat, cold, draughts, air contamination, and lighting conditions.
examples: sun exposure, hot/cold outdoor work, weather impacts
lighting issues (insufficient light, poor luminance, glare, flicker)

categories.label: Musculoskeletal strain
categories.description: Musculoskeletal strain from forced posture, awkward movement, lifting and carrying, repetitive work, or vibration.

categories.label: Psychosocial strain
categories.description: Psychosocial strain from overload, repetitive or one-sided tasks, low autonomy, emotional load, or adverse social conditions.
examples: overload (constant time pressure, sustained vigilance, excessive responsibility, misqualification)
highly repetitive work without cognitive engagement
monotone single-step tasks (only executing, only monitoring, takt-bound)
high emotional load from customer-facing work

categories.label: Unexpected actions (control / power failures)
categories.description: Unexpected actions from control or feedback loop failure, control malfunction, or loss of energy supply.
examples: loss of energy supply (short circuits, grid outages)

categories.label: Work organisation
categories.description: Work-organisation hazards from qualifications, information, instruction, interruptions, responsibilities, feedback, worker involvement, or working time.
examples: inadequate information/instruction, missing documentation, language barriers
lack of worker involvement, isolated workstations, poor planning

severity.label: Death
severity.anchor: Fatal injury or multiple severe injuries.

severity.label: Lost time injury
severity.anchor: Fracture or equivalent; days to weeks off work.

severity.label: First aid
severity.anchor: Minor injury; first aid only; no time off.

likelihood.label: Frequent
likelihood.anchor: At least monthly in a collective estimate of 1000 people performing the task under similar conditions.

likelihood.label: Occasional
likelihood.anchor: At least yearly and at most monthly in a collective estimate of 1000 people performing the task under similar conditions.

likelihood.label: Rare
likelihood.anchor: At least once every five years and at most yearly in a collective estimate of 1000 people performing the task under similar conditions.

likelihood.label: Unlikely
likelihood.anchor: At least once every 20 years and at most once every five years in a collective estimate of 1000 people performing the task under similar conditions.

likelihood.label: Practically impossible
likelihood.anchor: At least once every 100 years and at most once every 20 years in a collective estimate of 1000 people performing the task under similar conditions.
